channel.title: KiddieDaily — Science
channel.description: Kid-friendly science and nature news, bias-rated.

item.title: Scientists Create Tiny “Mini Livers” That Could One Day Replace Liver Transplants
item.description: Engineered tissue grafts could help perform key liver functions and benefit thousands of people living with liver failure. The liver is one of the body’s hardest-working organs, carrying out hun

item.title: 5 Things Princess Diana Loved That Might Surprise You
item.description: From one specific cocktail to astrology, here are five things you might not know Princess Diana was fond of.

item.title: Nautilus Array to Track Missing Exoplanet Atmospheres
item.description: Exoplanet atmospheres have become prima targets for astrobiologists in the search for life beyond Earth. This is because exoplanet surfaces can’t be directly imaged yet, so astronomers must get creati

item.title: Astronomers Discover Another Galaxy With No Dark Matter
item.description: Astronomers using W. M. Keck Observatory on Maunakea, Hawaiʻi Island, have discovered the third known galaxy apparently lacking dark matter, part of a strange linear structure that may have formed dur

item.title: The brain’s language network is more extensive than previously thought
item.description: A new study reveals that parts of the brain located far from the canonical language-processing centers are also involved in language comprehension.

item.title: Minute Man National Historical Park
item.description: Sites relevant to the start of the American Revolutionary War are interspersed throughout the modern-day Boston metropolitan region. The post Minute Man National Historical Park appeared first on NASA

item.title: Recent discoveries of ‘lost’ Mekong giant salmon carp renews hope for the fish
item.description: A large fish once feared extinct in Cambodia has been recorded in the country’s waters for the fourth time since 2020, renewing hope for the species. The Mekong giant salmon carp (Aaptosyax grypus), a

item.title: Scientists say Earth Impacts May Have Delivered Microbes to the Clouds of Venus
item.description: New models suggest Earth microbes may briefly survive in Venus’s clouds.

item.title: Habitable Worlds Targets in New Star Activity Catalog
item.description: Searching for habitable worlds beyond our solar system consists of more than just having it orbit within its star’s habitable zone, which is the region where temperatures could be just right for liqui

item.title: Iran leaves World Cup with warm send-off from Mexican fans
item.description: Mexican fans gathered in Tijuana to bid farewell to Iran's national football team before the squad departed

item.title: World Cup fans sweat through US 'heat dome'
item.description: As scorching heat left the US east coast baking in the middle of the World Cup, the BBC caught up with fans braving the weather to support their team.

item.title: ESA Outlines High-Tech Lander Instruments for 2050 Enceladus
item.description: Saturn’s moon, Enceladus, has become a prime solar system target for astrobiologists. This is because the small moon, which is just over 10 percent the diameter of Earth’s Moon, harbors a vast subsurf

item.title: Galaxy Groups Hiding in the Universe’s Emptiest Places
item.description: Even the universe’s emptiest regions, the vast voids that make up most of the volume of space, are not entirely empty. A new study using the CAVITY survey hunts for galaxy groups hiding within these v

item.title: Space Lasers Show How Venezuela’s Earthquakes Reshaped the Earth’s Crust
item.description: New satellite imagery reveals how much terrain has shifted in the wake of the twin quakes.

item.title: A Star’s Death Throes Involves a Lot of Kicking
item.description: When stars like the Sun reach the end of their lives, the textbook story has them puffing up and quietly shedding their outer layers to leave a white dwarf behind. A new model suggests it is far less

item.title: A portable ultrasound system could make reliable breast imaging more accessible
item.description: The new technology, which generates high-resolution, 3D images of breast tissue, requires no expertise to operate and could be used at home.

item.title: How urban design leads to better wellness
item.description: An extensive study of U.S. cities identifies walkable neighborhoods, urban greenery, and access to amenities as key contributors to residents’ health.

item.title: Pangolins are the world’s most trafficked mammal. A nearly 200-year-old specimen could help.
item.description: Their overlapping scales are believed to be a powerful aphrodisiac. The post Pangolins are the world’s most trafficked mammal. A nearly 200-year-old specimen could help. appeared first on Popula

item.title: Sun news: Bam! X flare! plus 5 M flares and a G1 geomagnetic storm
item.description: Sun news UPDATE for June 30, 2026: Bam!! X-flare!! Sunspot region AR4479 produced it. The post Sun news: Bam! X flare! plus 5 M flares and a G1 geomagnetic storm first appeared on EarthSky .

item.title: Two Lightning Bolts Collided Over a Japanese Tower and Triggered a Microburst of Nuclear-Level Radiation
item.description: An invisible, split-second blast reveals a new chapter in lightning physics.
